steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Before the blank: an independent clause — “improvements in efficiency often correlate negatively with resource conservation.”
After the blank: the word though followed by another clause about efficiency gains.
This is where a subordinating conjunction connects an independent clause to a clause that follows.

steps.title: Apply the rule for subordinating conjunctions
steps.content:
When a subordinating conjunction like though introduces a clause after an independent clause, use a comma before it and no punctuation after.
Options A, B, and D violate this by using semicolons or adding extra commas where they don't belong.
This matches choice C — C is correct.
